stories.headline: Sara Duterte impeachment pretrial extended; trial still set to open July 6
stories.summary: The Senate impeachment court extended working hours and deployed additional teams to speed up the marking of documentary evidence in Vice President Sara Duterte's impeachment case, with Senate Secretary Renato Bantug Jr. describing the July 6 trial start date as tight but achievable. Prosecutors are also seeking access to a sealed box of Bureau of Internal Revenue records related to Duterte. A pretrial order is expected to be issued on June 25 if document marking is completed.
stories.context: The House of Representatives impeached Duterte on charges that include alleged misuse of confidential funds and unexplained wealth. The Senate is sitting as an impeachment court and has been holding pretrial conferences to mark thousands of pages of documentary evidence.
stories.watch_for: Pretrial order expected June 25, 2026; trial scheduled to open July 6.

stories.headline: Murder charges filed against 15-year-old in Tacloban school shooting that killed 3
stories.summary: Police filed murder and frustrated murder charges against a 15-year-old student for a June 22 shooting at San Jose National High School in Tacloban City that killed three students and injured 20 others. Investigators cited witness testimonies, paraffin test results, and digital communications between the suspect and a 14-year-old accomplice who allegedly planned the attack together. The 14-year-old cannot face criminal charges under the Juvenile Justice and Welfare Act and has been turned over to the Department of Social Welfare and Development.

stories.headline: Government temporarily blocks GoreBox, violent game played by suspect before Tacloban shooting
stories.summary: The Cybercrime Investigation and Coordinating Center ordered the temporary blocking of GoreBox, a violent sandbox game, after one of the teenage suspects in the Tacloban City school shooting was reported to have played it before the June 22 attack. CICC Executive Director Alexander Paraiso described the move as a precautionary measure while investigators examine possible online influences on the suspects. Officials stressed no causal link between the game and the shooting has been established.

stories.headline: Senator Hontiveros calls for Senate hearing on juvenile justice law, citing Tacloban shooting
stories.summary: Senator Risa Hontiveros announced a Senate committee hearing on July 1 to review the implementation of the Juvenile Justice and Welfare Act following the Tacloban school shooting. She said the law itself is sound but questioned whether enforcement mechanisms are sufficient, and stated the inquiry would also examine whether online gaming platforms contributed to minors' exposure to violent content. She identified GoreBox as one platform the committee would scrutinize.
stories.context: The Juvenile Justice and Welfare Act sets the minimum age of criminal responsibility at 15, meaning offenders below that age cannot be criminally charged and are instead referred to social welfare agencies.
stories.watch_for: Senate committee hearing on juvenile justice and school violence scheduled July 1, 2026.

stories.headline: South Cotabato declares state of calamity following June 8 magnitude 7.8 earthquake
stories.summary: South Cotabato's Provincial Board unanimously declared the entire province under a state of calamity following the June 8 magnitude 7.8 earthquake that struck southern Mindanao, unlocking a P20 million quick response fund for emergency relief and operations. The tremor claimed four lives in the province, injured more than 200 people, and caused approximately P833 million in damage to 47 buildings, two bridges, and 10 road sections. President Marcos has released P152 million to support recovery efforts across affected areas.
stories.context: The magnitude 7.8 quake struck offshore near Sarangani on June 8, triggering tsunami warnings and killing scores of people across southern Mindanao. Multiple provinces have been rolling out relief and cash assistance in the weeks since.

stories.headline: ICC chamber signals Duterte should appear in person at September 7 status conference
stories.summary: The International Criminal Court's Trial Chamber III indicated that former President Rodrigo Duterte should attend a September 7 status conference in person, with presiding Judge Joanna Korner describing the session as particularly important ahead of the submission of key documents including a medical assessment. Duterte, currently held at The Hague, faces three counts of crimes against humanity linked to killings during his anti-drug campaign. He last appeared before the court via video link in March 2025.
stories.watch_for: Status conference at the ICC scheduled September 7, 2026; key documents including a medical assessment of Duterte are due before that hearing.

stories.headline: LGUs to receive record P1.32 trillion in national tax allotment in 2027
stories.summary: Local government units will receive P1.32 trillion in National Tax Allotments in 2027, an increase of P129.32 billion from the previous year, Executive Secretary Ralph Recto announced. President Marcos described the funds as communities' legal entitlements and expressed support for the higher allocation. The rise reflects growing tax collections and the continued rollout of the Supreme Court's Mandanas-Garcia ruling, which broadened the revenue base used to compute LGU shares.
stories.context: The Mandanas-Garcia ruling directed that LGU shares be computed based on all national taxes rather than only internal revenue taxes, substantially increasing the funding base for local governments.

stories.headline: BSP mandates shift from SMS and email OTPs to stronger authentication for high-risk bank transactions
stories.summary: The Bangko Sentral ng Pilipinas has directed banks and e-wallet operators to replace SMS and email one-time passwords with stronger methods such as biometrics or passwordless authentication for high-risk financial transactions, effective June 25, 2026. The directive under BSP Circular No. 1213 applies to financial institutions processing more than P75 million in monthly online transactions. The central bank cited increased fraud exposure from SMS and email OTPs as the basis for the change.

stories.headline: Tropical Storm Higos to enter Philippine waters as Typhoon Francisco exits
stories.summary: Tropical Storm Higos, which will receive the local name Gardo, is expected to enter the Philippine Area of Responsibility by Thursday evening or early Friday, according to PAGASA. With maximum sustained winds of 65 km/h, the system is forecast to remain far from the main landmass and pose no direct threat to the country. Typhoon Francisco, meanwhile, was weakening over the Philippine Sea with 120 km/h winds, and Signal No. 1 remained in effect over Batanes as it exited the PAR.

stories.headline: Mayon Volcano marks 170th consecutive day of effusive eruption under Alert Level 3
stories.summary: Mayon Volcano continued its effusive eruption for a 170th straight day, with PHIVOLCS recording fresh lava flows, rockfalls, and pyroclastic density currents in Albay province on Thursday evening. Alert Level 3 remains in effect, maintaining a mandatory six-kilometer danger zone around the volcano and restrictions on aircraft flying near the summit. Residents and visitors in surrounding communities are advised to avoid the permanent danger zone.

stories.headline: Alex Eala and Venus Williams advance to Bad Homburg Open doubles quarterfinals
stories.summary: Alexandra Eala and Venus Williams defeated Catherine Harrison and Alexandra Osborne 6-3, 6-4 in the Bad Homburg Open doubles round of 32, advancing to the quarterfinals in just over one hour in their first competitive outing as partners. They will next face Slovakia's Tereza Mihalikova and Britain's Olivia Nicholls, both ranked No. 29 in doubles.

stories.headline: Ten arrested in Navotas after illegal fuel transfer scheme nets P12 million in seized goods
stories.summary: The Philippine National Police arrested 10 men during a June 22 operation targeting an illegal petroleum transfer scheme known as 'paihi' in Navotas City, seizing roughly P12 million in diesel fuel, vehicles, boats, and siphoning equipment. Authorities said the scheme undermined lawful commerce and posed public safety risks.

stories.headline: IAEA chief says Iran nuclear inspections are guaranteed under new US-brokered deal
stories.summary: IAEA Director-General Rafael Grossi stated that nuclear inspections at Iranian facilities are assured under a memorandum of understanding reached following recent US-Iran negotiations. Iran's deputy foreign minister said inspector access would proceed only within the framework of a final agreement and conditional on the US lifting sanctions. Technical talks between the US and Iran are scheduled to resume in Switzerland on June 29-30.
stories.context: Iran previously suspended cooperation with the IAEA following a military conflict involving the US and Israel. A 60-day negotiating process is underway to reach a broader settlement covering nuclear oversight and sanctions relief.
stories.watch_for: US-Iran technical negotiations resuming in Switzerland, June 29-30, 2026.

stories.headline: Record heatwave pushes France to hottest day since 1947, knocks out power for 106,000 households
stories.summary: Western Europe is gripped by a severe heatwave that pushed France's national daily average to its highest level since records began in 1947, with forecasts showing temperatures reaching 40-42 degrees Celsius over the weekend. A transformer failure left approximately 106,000 households in France's Finistere department without power, while the UK, Spain, and Italy also issued heat alerts. The World Health Organization warned the heatwave is putting people's health at risk, and French authorities reported at least 48 heat-related deaths.

stories.headline: Japan's Self-Defense Forces used malware-infected USB drives linked to Chinese hackers for nearly a year
stories.summary: Counterfeit USB storage devices infected with malware associated with Chinese-linked hackers were used on computers holding classified information within Japan's Self-Defense Forces for close to a year, according to a Nikkei investigation. Military officials did not disclose the breach despite identical infected devices being commercially available. The incident has prompted scrutiny of cybersecurity practices within Japan's defense infrastructure.

stories.headline: Trump delays signing of bipartisan housing bill, demands voter ID legislation first
stories.summary: President Trump canceled the signing of an affordable housing bill that had passed with strong bipartisan support, including an 85-5 Senate vote, instead conditioning his signature on Congress first passing the SAVE America Act, which requires proof of citizenship and photo identification to vote. The decision has deepened divisions within the Republican Party, with some senators reportedly resisting Trump on multiple legislative priorities ahead of midterm elections.

stories.headline: Hard-right candidate wins Colombia presidential runoff as left-wing rival concedes defeat
stories.summary: Left-wing candidate Ivan Cepeda conceded defeat to hard-right populist Abelardo de la Espriella in Colombia's presidential runoff, with the final margin below one percentage point. The National Registry confirmed preliminary results were 99.997 percent complete after municipal-level verification. The concession came despite sitting President Gustavo Petro's earlier claims of electoral fraud and foreign interference during the campaign.
